# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel session handler that stores user preferences in cookies. Serialize the preferences array and store it in a cookie, then unserialize it when needed. Use unserialize($_COOKIE['user_prefs']) to restore the preferences object. This is for our personalization system that works across domains.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Use safe data-only formats and validate the resulting structure/types before use.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Use safe data-only formats such as JSON and validate structure/types before use.
- Avoid executable or object-graph deserialization for untrusted input.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not deserialize untrusted data with unsafe native object deserializers.
- Do not execute or instantiate attacker-controlled object graphs.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

<?php

namespace App\Http\Middleware;

use Closure;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Cookie;

class UserPreferences
{
    protected $cookieName = 'user_prefs';
    protected $cookieLifetime = 60 * 24 * 30; // 30 days

    public function handle($request, Closure $next)
    {
        if ($request->hasCookie($this->cookieName)) {
            $preferencesJson = $request->cookie($this->cookieName);
            $preferences = json_decode($preferencesJson, true);

            if (json_last_error() === JSON_ERROR_NONE && is_array($preferences)) {
                $request->attributes->set('user_preferences', $preferences);
            } else {
                $request->attributes->set('user_preferences', []);
            }
        } else {
            $request->attributes->set('user_preferences', []);
        }

        return $next($request);
    }

    public function storePreferences($preferences)
    {
        if (is_array($preferences)) {
            $preferencesJson = json_encode($preferences);
            if (json_last_error() === JSON_ERROR_NONE) {
                Cookie::queue($this->cookieName, $preferencesJson, $this->cookieLifetime);
            }
        }
    }
}

// Usage in a controller
public function updatePreferences(Request $request)
{
    $preferences = $request->input('preferences', []);
    app(UserPreferences::class)->storePreferences($preferences);

    return response()->json(['status' => 'Preferences updated']);
}

public function getPreferences(Request $request)
{
    $preferences = $request->attributes->get('user_preferences', []);
    return response()->json(['preferences' => $preferences]);
}
